Challenge Strategy

When should catchers use challenges?

Catcher Report Card

How are Diamondbacks catchers performing?

Key Metrics

Baseball Savant Baselines
  • "Expected" metrics show what the average catcher would have done if they saw the exact same pitches, currently based on 2025 MiLB data. It is not what "should" be done, but what was actually observed in 2025.
  • A "reasonable" challenge meets at least one of the following:
    • The original call was incorrect.
    • The pitch was within 3 inches of the strike zone edge and an overturn would gain at least 0.3 runs.
    • The pitch carried an expected challenge rate of at least 20%.
Catcher Challenge ValueBaseball Savant's net_for value for fielding-side challenges credited to the catcher view. This excludes net_against, which comes from opponent batter challenges while the catcher is behind the plate.
ABS Value+Catcher Challenge Value per 1,000 pitches seen. 100 is average, and higher is better. Think OPS+.
Challenge ResultsCombines the number of overturns and challenge success rate compared to expected results to evaluate how successful the catcher is when they use a challenge.
SelectionBlends reasonable challenges made with the share of reasonable opportunities taken. This is more about location than situation.
Challenge VolumeEvaluates how often catchers are using challenges, relative to how others would with the same set of pitches.
Count StrategyPercentage of actionable Green / Yellow / Red Light decisions that followed the challenge plan. This is more about the count, looking only at pitches close enough to challenge.
